query:
SELECT 
  p.id,
  p.name,
  p.location,
  p.priority,
  p.stage,
  p.value,
  p.timeline,
  p.lifecycleStatus,
  LEFT(p.overview, 300) as overview,
  LEFT(p.opportunityNote, 250) as opportunityNote,
  COUNT(DISTINCT cp.contactId) as totalContacts,
  SUM(CASE WHEN c.enrichmentStatus = 'enriched' AND c.email IS NOT NULL AND c.email != '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as sendReady,
  SUM(CASE WHEN c.enrichmentStatus = 'pending' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as pendingEnrichment,
  SUM(CASE WHEN c.enrichmentStatus = 'not_found' OR c.enrichmentStatus = 'failed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as notFound
FROM projects p
LEFT JOIN contactProjects cp ON cp.projectId = p.id
LEFT JOIN contacts c ON c.id = cp.contactId
WHERE JSON_CONTAINS(p.matchedBusinessLines, '90002')
AND p.priority = 'warm'
AND p.lifecycleStatus NOT IN ('stale', 'cancelled', 'completed')
GROUP BY p.id, p.name, p.location, p.priority, p.stage, p.value, p.timeline, p.lifecycleStatus, p.overview, p.opportunityNote
ORDER BY p.id;
command:
mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user HWMKhVWm4R1gayY.38561a93bc5c --database 3SMu786VMCWdCnmNSx6pxw --execute 
SELECT 
  p.id,
  p.name,
  p.location,
  p.priority,
  p.stage,
  p.value,
  p.timeline,
  p.lifecycleStatus,
  LEFT(p.overview, 300) as overview,
  LEFT(p.opportunityNote, 250) as opportunityNote,
  COUNT(DISTINCT cp.contactId) as totalContacts,
  SUM(CASE WHEN c.enrichmentStatus = 'enriched' AND c.email IS NOT NULL AND c.email != '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as sendReady,
  SUM(CASE WHEN c.enrichmentStatus = 'pending' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as pendingEnrichment,
  SUM(CASE WHEN c.enrichmentStatus = 'not_found' OR c.enrichmentStatus = 'failed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as notFound
FROM projects p
LEFT JOIN contactProjects cp ON cp.projectId = p.id
LEFT JOIN contacts c ON c.id = cp.contactId
WHERE JSON_CONTAINS(p.matchedBusinessLines, '90002')
AND p.priority = 'warm'
AND p.lifecycleStatus NOT IN ('stale', 'cancelled', 'completed')
GROUP BY p.id, p.name, p.location, p.priority, p.stage, p.value, p.timeline, p.lifecycleStatus, p.overview, p.opportunityNote
ORDER BY p.id;

rows.name: Chevron Australia Operations — NWS & Gorgon
rows.stage: Active — 0 open, 92 awarded, 38 closed work packages
rows.overview: Operations and maintenance of Chevron's North West Shelf and Gorgon LNG facilities. Includes subsea, inlet processing, production, utilities, and storage/offloading. Ongoing maintenance and turnaround opportunities.
rows.opportunityNote: ICN Gateway project with 131 work packages (0 open, 92 awarded). LNG plant turnarounds require fleet-scale compressed air

rows.name: Viva Energy LNG Terminal
rows.overview: Viva Energy's proposed liquefied natural gas (LNG) terminal in Geelong has received federal environmental approval, marking a key milestone for the project.
rows.opportunityNote: Construction of an LNG terminal will require significant civil works. Potential for portable air compressors for various construction tasks, generators for site power, and lighting towers. Dewatering pumps might be needed for foundation work or if lo

rows.name: Pluto LNG Flaring Technology Upgrade
rows.overview: Woodside is installing new flaring technology at its Pluto LNG facility. The upgrade is expected to be installed at Pluto 2 later this year. This project aims to improve operational efficiency and potentially reduce emissions.
rows.opportunityNote: Monitor for potential rental opportunities for temporary power, lighting, or compressed air during installation, commissioning, or maintenance activities at the Pluto LNG facility. The project is an upgrade, not a new build, so direct CAPEX for major

rows.name: Gorgon Backfill Fields Development
rows.overview: Chevron plans to drill six new wells to maintain production at the Gorgon LNG facility, safeguarding Australia's energy security.
rows.opportunityNote: Chevron is the owner. Potential for direct sales of drilling compressors, generators, and potentially pumps for dewatering/process needs.

rows.name: Pluto LNG Facility Operations
rows.timeline: Ongoing operations, potential for immediate disruption
rows.overview: UGL workers at Woodside's Pluto LNG facility are voting on a pay offer and potential industrial action. The facility is a major LNG production hub.
rows.opportunityNote: Monitor for potential disruptions due to industrial action which may lead to increased demand for rental equipment or temporary power solutions. UGL is a key contractor for follow-up.

rows.name: Ichthys LNG Project Operations
rows.timeline: Ongoing operations, potential for immediate disruption
rows.overview: Workers at INPEX's Ichthys LNG Project have voted in favour of strike action, indicating ongoing industrial relations issues. The project is a large-scale LNG production facility.
rows.opportunityNote: Monitor for potential disruptions due to industrial action which may lead to increased demand for rental equipment or temporary power solutions.

rows.name: Browse LNG Project
rows.timeline: Not specified, currently in cost estimation phase.
rows.overview: Woodside expects its Browse LNG project, which would deliver natural gas from the Calliance, Torosa, and Brecknock fields to the existing Karratha gas plant, to cost A$48.7 billion ($35.2 billion).
rows.opportunityNote: Large-scale LNG project with significant CAPEX. Potential for power generation, portable air for construction/maintenance, and pumps. Monitor for FID and contractor awards.

rows.name: SA Water network expansion to Wellington East
rows.overview: Around 220 homes in Wellington East will gain access to safe, treated drinking water as the township’s supply is connected to the wider SA Water network. The upgrade involves expanding the network.
rows.opportunityNote: SA Water is expanding its network to connect Wellington East homes to treated drinking water. This will involve pipeline installation and potentially other infrastructure upgrades. Opportunity for pumps (dewatering during trenching, potentially boost

rows.name: Ichthys LNG Export Terminal Operations
rows.overview: Workers at the Inpex-operated Ichthys liquefied natural gas (LNG) export terminal have started industrial action over pay and conditions. This indicates ongoing operational activity at the facility.
rows.opportunityNote: Monitor the situation for potential operational disruptions or maintenance needs that might require temporary power, air, or dewatering solutions. Industrial action could lead to increased demand for rental equipment.

rows.name: Geelong LNG Import Terminal
rows.timeline: Ongoing approvals, construction timeline not yet specified.
rows.overview: Viva Energy has secured another environmental approval for its proposed FSRU-based LNG import terminal in Geelong, Victoria. This project involves importing liquefied natural gas via a Floating Storage and Regasification Unit (FSRU) to supply the Australian market.
rows.opportunityNote: This project is in the approval stage, indicating future construction and equipment needs. Atlas Copco PT can target the project owner (Viva Energy) for direct CAPEX opportunities for power generation, pumps, and potentially BESS. Once EPC or constru
